sentences.text: Hi, Henry.
How's it going?
Good, good.
How are you?
I'm good.
Working from home today?
Yep.
Little, little… Well, actually, I'm at my parents' vacation rental, they're not here, so….
Oh, no, I swear.
We're just, in Amaganza?
Fairness.
Yeah.
Very nice.
Okay, have you had a chance to log into the tool yet?
I've picked in to it a little bit, … I think it's okay.
I will say this.
I'm sure it's, like, very clever and very good.
It wasn't accurate to the data that we have in our system.
Oh, interesting.
Maybe we're wrong.
I don't think so, … I don't think so.
… You know, it was, like, relatively correct, right, within a percentage point or two.
Okay.
As far as what the occupancy was, … Number of applications, that sort of thing.
… So….
So, like, here, under vacant, like, were these metrics… did you find this customized metrics, and did you toggle some of this on?
Yeah, well, I was just looking at, percentage leased.
Okay.
… You know, percentage of these, percentage.
number of vacant units, that sort of stuff.
It wasn't, like, perfect.
And…
at least from my understanding from the demo, it was that, like, hey, we're fucking on it.
Whether it's within a percentage point or two, you know, it does matter in this business, but maybe not so much in this sense.
So, for, like, vacant, for instance, you said vacant units was off.
Are you… is your definition of vacant, so all of our metrics here have a tooltip, is your definition of vacant the same as ours?
So that's something to take into consideration, so this is within the next 10 days.
Okay, I'll double check that.
Okay.
Yeah, I can double-check those definitions.
Maybe we're looking at slightly different things.
Okay.
… But I mean, like, it is… it is….
Did you do these comps, or did we?
No, you did.
You did.
Okay.
Do you know how to….
Goodness.
And all that kind of stuff.
Sorry, do I know how to edit the comps?
Yeah.
No, I don't.
Okay, so I'll just show you some basic functionality, so when you continue to explore the tool, you just know some, like, basic things.
So up here in the top right, you have full control over the comps, so you can add and remove comps as often as you'd like.
This is your property.

The white pins, also over here on the side, the white pins are currently in your comp set, black pins are other properties in the area that you could consider adding, and you can simply add them from here.
So you could say add comp, and it will instantly be added.
Likewise, over on the left-hand side, you can X off any comp that you don't want in your comp set, and you can either add it from the map view, or we have the list view over here.
However, if you don't see a property, you can also add comp and type in the name of the property here as well.
So this, you have full autonomy over editing your comps and all that kind of stuff, which is cool.
Search by class, and all that kind of stuff.
So there's that.
You found this Customize Metrics button, so you can toggle on any metrics that you actually… that you deem important, so you can look at whatever metrics you want to look at.
You have the different filtering options, so you can look at it based on different bedrooms, bathrooms.
Just know that with these filters, whatever you have toggled on here, so whatever the view is that you have here.
When you go to export in Excel, either have it send to an inbox, or if you download it, it's going to reflect what you currently have toggled on in the app.
So just know that as well.
Nevertheless.
If you want additional users to have access, down here on the left-hand side, manage users, you can add Other people, so if there's other… Asset managers, you want to take a look at the tool.
You just invite new user, type in their email address.
Right now, you have admin access, I mean, it's not a big deal in the… In this instance, because it's obviously a demo account.
Sure.
But you'll be able to obviously add users, edit comps, and all that kind of stuff.
That's what the admin access is.
So, if you want to add additional people, feel free to do that.
Perfect.
I did turn on Explore for you, so you guys can look at that market and sub-market data.

So, I'm up in the Northeast, you type in any neighborhood zip code, you can even type in a property address, and it will pull within a certain radius, but this will give you… The market overview as to what's going on in the market, once it loads.
Very slowly.
It's coming.

So here, obviously, in Boston, we're looking at about 5,000 properties, 420,000 units, and then you can use the different filtering options at the top as well to filter out what's relevant to you guys.
So you have these two options.
But, okay, so interested so far.
So you don't think it's directly on point, but you'll look at, you'll look at some of the metrics and actually… Take a look at that.
As a reminder, you can drill into these, so you can look at the 11 units that we're saying are available within the next 10 days.
Either are currently available or will be available within the next 10 days.
Okay.
And you can do that not only for your property, but your comps as well.
The pricing and availability, broken down by floor plan.
Fees and deposits… Amenities and unit community.
So overall, at this point, you're saying that you're not wowed by it.
I mean, it's great.
And it's… it's pretty tricky.
I… I'm not…
Listen, like, in the workflow of my teams, if I take away them doing comp data, like, it throws off other stuff that I think is valuable, right?
Like… Focusing on what the competition is doing through doing these sorts of tasks.
helps my team… come up with ideas on how to manage the properties better.
It also instills a better sense of competitiveness, which I think leads to better outcomes.
So psychologically, I think going through and doing the market studies Irregardless of how super accurate they are, right?
Like.
We test rents all the time.
If we can get them, we're gonna get them.
But it has all these other… positives that if I take that out of their job role, or I make it less important.
… They're not doing.
And… I kinda like that they do that.
Right now, because… You know, when we're talking about where rents are.
And, oh, what's going on with those other properties?
It gives a chance for… you know, my PM teams to better understand.
better understand, like, what… what the other people are doing, how they answer the phones, how they're interacting with potential people, who… who's doing what, right?
Like, if they used to work at another competitor.
… It just sort of helps them see the market better, and… part of the problem, I think, fundamentally.
with property management is they don't, … it's not, it's not given the, like, I think…
attention it's always deserved?

And it's not to say, like, they can't keep doing their own calls, but if they see that I'm just getting data this way, well, they're not going to be as incentivized, because I don't actually need the stuff anymore, right?
got the comps, I'm getting the information up to date.
Okay, so-and-so is doing this.
Okay, great.
Let's just move the pricing, but… …
I don't know.
That's… that's… that's kind of the way I read it right now.
I… I don't know, maybe it's old school, maybe it's an antiquated way of thinking about things, maybe I'm wrong.
But… you know, it only really would make sense in the market rate side.
The market rate side is a smaller piece.
It's definitely very important.
But, … How many… do you know how many units are on the market rate side?
I know you gave us a range total for the whole portfolio, it's between 26,000 and 28,000 units.
Yeah, about 8,000 market rate.
Okay, so I just had a call yesterday, with a company that's moving forward with us, and to your point, they're still having their on-site teams do the call-arounds.
They're more so using this, basically, one, to just have an eye on oh, shoot, our competitor down the street just had 3 leases this week, what are they doing?
Let's pick up the phone, or let's go and physically go shop that property to see what's going on.
And another one is when they have A potential customer come in.

and they say, well, I'm also considering this property down the street, and it's cheaper, they can quickly look at this and be like, okay, well, it's also… the square footage is smaller, and we also offer these amenities, so they're using it more as a sales tactic to be like, okay, like, more informational of, well, you know.
that makes sense.
It might be a little bit cheaper, but our unit's bigger, and we have all of these other amenities that they don't currently offer.
So, they're using it in that capacity as well.
Oh, that's an interesting use case, didn't think about it like that.

And that was literally a conversation I just had yesterday, so… and we do a lot of… I bet these conversations are negating the manual market surveys, they're still having their teams do it for the knowledge aspect, and to make sure, like, they're not missing anything, but this is more so, like… a quick glimpse of, are we missing something that we should have had our eye on, and, like, this is gonna flag something if, like, our radar isn't on, so….
Yeah, you're right, but people are lazy.
Yeah.
And… … my, you know, my teams are, like, they're always complaining, we got too much to do, we got too much of this, right?
So, like.
They're out there at these properties, they're in an office with two other people.
The regional comes sometimes.
The VPs are there, you know, probably once a month.
Right.
Unless it's a, you know, some… some… for whatever reason, they're even there more.
… If they can go on the website and say, blah blah blah, here's the data.
Got it, send it away, done.
Now I've saved an hour or two hours a week of hauling around, trying to figure this stuff out.
They're gonna do that.
Like, that's what I would do.
I know, right?
So it's like… It's kind of one or the other, in my mind, right?
If I do this, I don't really make them do that, because it's…
That would then, like.
you can talk all you want, culture of excellence, culture of this, that, or the third, whatever it is, right?
Like, getting them to do that.
You know, extra 10% that, you know, provides some sort of intangible value.
It's never gonna happen.
… I don't know.
So… I don't know, that's… that's kind of what… My thoughts are right now.
You know, you had brought up the use case of the development side.
we're really only developing, in our pipeline actively right now, affordable deals.
You know, those pretty much always get the max rents.
If they don't, that's when we get more involved, but… We'd be getting the information we need to get there anyways.
Yeah.
And it's not worth it for, you know, 50 cents a unit across the portfolio.
So… I… I do think they're… You know, there is a world where this makes sense.
… But… I just don't… I don't see me going into my boss's office and saying, hey, listen.
For even 25 cents a unit a month.
we could get this interesting information, they would say, well, we like that our teams are doing this right now, right?
We're integrating a couple other platforms right now, to help.
Take some time back.
Elise AI and… app works.
One is, like, a maintenance side thing, and one is, like, a marketing and leasing thing.
… And they should be providing the teams a lot more time, frankly.
So…

even, rolling something like this out in the organized way that it ought to be, with the trainings and that sort of stuff, I don't think it would work in the timeline, and we're already, you know, integrating a couple extra, you know.
20 cents a unit, 70 cents a unit, a dollar a unit, whatever it is.
Yeah.
I don't roll off the top of my head.
So, I… I also don't think now is just, like.
gut feeling, are we going to be ready to make this spend?
I don't think we're there.
… Okay.
But… Maybe that changes in the future.
Like I said, you know, our asset management team is young.
I'm still gonna show this to the regional at the Aurora, who I really like, she's fantastic.
And maybe she's gonna look at this differently than me.
So I'll add her as a user, and hopefully she's got some time, she can dig in there and say, actually, this would be really good.
I'm not so sure this is, you know, she could think differently from me.
a lot longer than I have, so… … And to the VP who oversees her as well.
So, we'll… I'll… I'll send it off to them, I'll see what they have to think, and if they feel differently from me, I won't tell them what I think.
Then I can ping you again.
Okay.
Yeah.

One thing I do ask of you is, as you're going through the platform, or as your regional, like, looks at the data, if you're seeing discrepancies, if you could send that to me, that would be helpful, because, as I mentioned, everything that we're getting, we're getting from the website, so… If you're seeing inaccurate data, I'd like to pass it on to my team to look into, at least, on the back end, just to understand the differences of what you're seeing versus what we're reporting to you.
And then, second to that is, you mentioned about the cost and all that kind of stuff.
one, it doesn't sound like you're completely sold on the tool anyway, so cost shouldn't be an issue, like, regardless.

But if it does eventually come down to the cost, and you're like, you know what, I do like this, I could see some value, but that price point is either A too high, we can work with you on that, that price point allows for unlimited users.
We also have an option which allows for limited users, so it's up to 5 users, and it's $15,000 annually, so it's a lot less, but obviously you have a limited user bucket, so….
Hmm.
So just keep that in mind as well, … Okay.

There are a couple options, but typically, portfolio of even 8,000 units, if this did roll out across the portfolio with the regionals and on-site teams, it typically makes the most sense, obviously, to do the unlimited user option, just… Because… well, if you want everybody to have access, but just know that that's available, but first and foremost, I think it would be helpful just if whatever data discrepancies you see or find, it would be helpful if you pass that over to me so I could relay that to our team.
Absolutely.
How much time do you want with the tool?
Other week?
Maybe two?
Okay, that's totally fine.
Yeah, awesome.
Cool.
I, if I haven't heard from you, I'll plan on reaching out to you, just as a quick touchpoint.
I'll send you an email, maybe back end of next week, just to see how things are going, questions or anything like that.
If the regional that you add to the account wants to see a demo and, like, us walk her through that, we're more than happy to set that up as well.
Great.
Thank you.
I appreciate it.
Of course.
So, just let me know if you need anything, let me know if questions come up, but I'll plan on reaching back out to you next week, just as a quick touchpoint.
Awesome.
Awesome.
Thanks, Jamie.
Thanks, Henry.
Take care.
Take care, bye now.
